title: barringtonia lanceolata
intro: Barringtonia lanceolata, also known as the sea poison tree or lanceolate barringtonia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lecythidaceae, native to the coastal regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Barringtonia lanceolata include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections and promote wound healing. Barringtonia lanceolata is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it can also be used in the form of a paste or cream for topical application, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
